title: Factory Raises $150M for Enterprise AI Infrastructure - LinkedIn

full_text:
Factory Raises $150M for Enterprise AI Infrastructure
This title was summarized by AI from the post below.
Tech Funding News
65,110 followers
2mo
Report this post
🏭 AI coding tools are everywhere. Factory is building something different: the infrastructure that makes AI agents actually reliable in enterprise engineering environments.
The startup has raised $150 million in Series C funding at a $1.5 billion valuation, led by Khosla Ventures , with Sequoia Capital , Insight Partners , Blackstone , and others participating.
Founded in 2023 by Matan Grinberg and Eno Reyes , Factory developed Droids: AI agents that go far beyond code generation to handle testing, review, documentation, and deployment across the entire software development lifecycle.
👉 Read the full story: https://lnkd.in/daZDz4mT
#AI #SoftwareDevelopment #EnterpriseAI #AIAgents #VentureCapital #Startups #TechFundingNews #DeveloperTools

65,110 followers
1mo
Edited
Report this post
💰 Blitzy that uses AI to autonomously complete large-scale software development projects for major enterprises has raised $200 million in a growth funding round at a $1.4 billion valuation.
The round was led by Northzone , with participation from PSG , Battery Ventures , Jump Capital , Morgan Creek Digital, and Defiant . Existing backers, including Flybridge , Link Ventures , NFX , Picus Capital , and Venture Guides , alongside strategic investors Liberty Mutual Strategic Ventures , Erie Strategic Ventures, and BAL Ventures joined the round.

Keith Jordan
2mo
Report this post
Factory, a startup developing AI agents for enterprise engineering teams, announced it had raised $150 million at a $1.5 billion valuation. The round was led by Khosla Ventures, with participation from Sequoia Capital, Insight Partners, and Blackstone. Keith Rabois, a managing director at Khosla Ventures, joined the startup’s board.
Factory founder Matan Grinberg told the Wall Street Journal that the company’s key differentiator is its ability to switch between different foundation models, such as Anthropic’s Claude or Chinese AI startup DeepSeek. However, startups like Cursor also don’t rely on a single model to generate code.
Factory’s customers include engineering teams at Morgan Stanley, Ernst & Young, and Palo Alto Networks.

Artificial intelligence startup Cursor is in talks to raise a $2 billion fundraising round at an over $50 billion valuation, which does not include the investment, a source familiar with the matter told CNBC .
Andreessen Horowitz is slated to co-lead the new investment round, in which NVIDIA and Thrive Capital are also expected to participate, said the source, who was not authorized to publicly speak on the investment. Those three firms have all previously backed the AI coding startup.

Artificial intelligence startup Cursor is in talks to raise a $2 billion fundraising round at an over $50 billion valuation, which does not include the investment, a source familiar with the matter told CNBC.
Andreessen Horowitz is slated to co-lead the new investment round, in which Nvidia and Thrive Capital are also expected to participate, said the source, who was not authorized to publicly speak on the investment. Those three firms have all previously backed the AI coding startup.

CodeWords , a London-based AI agent platform, has raised a €7.6 million ($9 million) Seed funding round to expand its engineering and go-to-market teams.
CodeWords, founded by Aymeric Zhuo and Osman Ramadan , builds Cody, an AI agent that learns how a business works and takes action proactively. Cody automates repetitive tasks, runs them continuously on CodeWords’ infrastructure, and improves over time, with no coding, deployment, or maintenance required.

AI coding startup Cursor is nearing new funding in which the four-year-old company would raise at least $2 billion in fresh capital, according to four sources familiar with the matter. Returning investors Thrive and Andreessen Horowitz are expected to lead the financing at a $50 billion valuation, prior to the new capital injection.

CodeWords , a London-based AI agent platform, has raised a €7.6 million ($9 million) Seed funding round to expand its engineering and go-to-market teams.
CodeWords, founded by Aymeric Zhuo and Osman Ramadan , builds Cody, an AI agent that learns how a business works and takes action proactively. Cody automates repetitive tasks, runs them continuously on CodeWords’ infrastructure, and improves over time, with no coding, deployment, or maintenance required.
